subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF Art IV, OECD, MONEYVAL, Freedom House, RSF, EU) + international/European press.

A stable co-principality (the French President and the Bishop of Urgell as co-princes) with an elected parliament; a tourism/retail/banking economy diversifying after banking-secrecy reform, using the euro.

Standout features: strong institutions, a prudent fiscal rule, top-tier safety, and an EU-Association-Agreement upside (concluded at Council level Dec 2025, pending ratification + a referendum), against rapid aging and a thin tax base.

economic.notes: A prudent, low-debt, diversifying economy with an EU-association upside; euro-using, no monetary autonomy.

fiscal_state.notes: A fiscal rule caps the deficit at 1% of GDP and debt at 40%, with resilient ~2.1% growth and a large current-account surplus. (IMF Art IV 2025/2026)

monetary_independence.notes: Uses the euro under an EU monetary agreement - no own monetary policy, imports the ECB stance. (EU monetary agreement)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Uses the euro (a reserve currency) but issues none of it. (IMF; EU monetary agreement)

inflation.notes: Subsiding (~2.6% end-2024), tracking the eurozone despite a tight labour market. (IMF Art IV 2025)

banking_stability.notes: Banks profitable on high margins/fees; the post-secrecy-reform sector is sound but small and concentrated. (IMF Art IV 2025)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A diversifying tourism/retail/banking economy; the EU Association Agreement would deepen single-market access - small but capable, gaining optionality if ratified. (EC; IMF)

institutional.notes: Strong rule of law and a free press (with the usual microstate data-thinness).

rule_of_law.notes: Strong, stable rule of law - 'Free' (FH 93/100); no dedicated WJP coverage caps confidence. (Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Credible official statistics and IMF-engaged, with thin coverage vs Eurostat-grade systems. (IMF Art IV)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A competent small administration, with the EU-accession workstream straining a thin civil service. (EC; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: RSF #5 globally (2025) - among the world's freest and independent, though a tiny media market. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: A stable democracy with protected liberties, against a long-run aging drag.

political_stability.notes: A stable co-principality with an elected parliament and regular free/fair elections. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: >50% of residents are foreign; integration is peaceful, but strict naturalization leaves most non-citizens without voting rights. (Freedom House 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: A cohesive, low-conflict society despite ~100 nationalities, with housing/cost pressure as population nears 90k. (Andorra statistics; FH)

demographics.notes: Immigration-fed growth now, but fertility ~0.8 and rapid aging make the long term a fiscal/growth drag. (Andorra statistics; IMF)

geopolitical.notes: A demilitarized, conflict-free state under Franco-Spanish protection, EU-associating.

alliance_reliability.notes: Not in NATO/EU, but with deep Franco-Spanish protection and a pending EU Association Agreement. (EC; UN)

conflict_involvement.notes: No conflict exposure - landlocked and demilitarized. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account aligned with EU/international norms post-secrecy-reform. (IMF; EC)

physical_practical.notes: Among the world's safest, with strong healthcare, against ski-tourism climate sensitivity.

climate.notes: Alpine/Pyrenean - low catastrophe exposure but ski tourism is sensitive to long-term warming/snowpack. (general)

healthcare.notes: Very high life expectancy (~84+) and a strong universal system, with complex cases referred to Spain/France. (WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Good for its size, but landlocked, road-dependent, and with no airport/rail of its own. (general)

crime_safety.notes: Among the safest countries in the world. (national crime stats)

disaster_insurance.notes: Developed insurance access via Spain/France, with low catastrophe exposure. (general)

summary: A very safe, high-income, well-governed co-principality with strong institutions (FH 93/100, RSF #5), a resilient diversifying tourism/retail/banking economy, a prudent fiscal rule, and very high life expectancy. The headline upside is the EU Association Agreement concluded at Council level in December 2025, which would grant near-EEA single-market access - but it still needs EU national ratifications and an Andorran referendum where opinion is roughly split, so it is upside, not a done deal. The main long-run risk is demographic: ultra-low fertility and rapid aging will erode growth and load pension/healthcare costs. It uses the euro with no monetary autonomy; overall positively skewed and stable.
